spec_reconstruction.domain: Current U.S. federal banking board obligations for Category I global systemically important bank holding companies and their insured depository institution subsidiaries.
spec_reconstruction.unit_of_analysis: Each discrete current, in-force board-level obligation, with source, duty, actor, committee or director-type allocation, and reform relevance.
structural_axes: entity tier: Category I GSIB BHC versus insured depository institution subsidiary
actor: full board versus board committee
committee: audit, risk, compensation, nominating/governance, and any other required committee
director type: inside, outside, independent, non-independent, and risk-expert distinctions
source tier: statute, regulation, enforceable guideline, supervisory guidance, examination manual, interagency standard
issuing authority: Federal Reserve, OCC, FDIC, CFPB, FFIEC/interagency

regulatory domain: capital, stress testing, liquidity, resolution, recovery, operational resilience, model risk, third-party risk, incentive compensation, cybersecurity, BSA/AML, consumer compliance, safety and soundness
actor_classes_required: Category I GSIB BHC full board
source_universe: Federal Reserve regulations and supervision letters/guidance
OCC regulations, enforceable guidelines, bulletins, and Comptroller's Handbook material
FDIC regulations, guidance, and examination material
CFPB supervision and examination material relevant to bank consumer compliance oversight
Relevant federal statutes underlying those regulations and guidance
coverage_areas: audit, reporting, insider, affiliate, fiduciary, and corporate governance obligations
spec_reconstruction.audience_use: Bank directors, counsel, and policymakers seeking a granular working document for reform and reduction of duplicative or burdensome board requirements.
spec_reconstruction.form_depth_expectations: Extensive 40-60 page, citation-level inventory with granular pin cites, actor mapping, current-law status, and reform-useful analysis.

material_omissions: No systematic BHC-versus-IDI subsidiary tiering; the workbook says its lens is 'Primarily the national bank / federal savings association view (OCC)'.
No required actor column mapping duties to full board, risk committee, audit committee, compensation committee, nominating/governance committee, or director type.
Nominating/governance committee obligations are essentially absent; compensation committee content is thin and mostly securities-law oriented.
Category I GSIB-specific obligations are mixed with all-bank, national-bank, public-company, smaller-institution, trust, and de novo bank material without a filter.
Consumer compliance, CRA, fair lending, and flood-insurance rows are mostly generic oversight assertions rather than citation-level board duties.
IDI resolution planning under FDIC 12 CFR 360.10 is missing while BHC resolution planning is covered.
Recovery planning is treated as current even though OCC rescinded 12 CFR part 30 Appendix E before June 2026.
The workbook is a compact 66-row spreadsheet, not the requested 40-60 page granular inventory.

results.reason: US Code supports the 5-25 national-bank director band; source verified via U.S. Code House site: https://uscode.house.gov/
US Code supports qualifying-share and citizenship/residency director qualifications for national banks; source verified via U.S. Code House site: https://uscode.house.gov/

Federal Reserve Regulation YY requires a risk committee, committee charter, independent chair, and risk-management framework oversight: https://www.federalreserve.gov/frrs/regulations/section-25233-risk-management-and-risk-committee-requirements.htm
Federal Reserve SR 21-3 sets five board-effectiveness attributes for large domestic BHCs/SLHCs >= $100B: https://www.federalreserve.gov/supervisionreg/srletters/SR2103.htm

OCC heightened standards support board/risk-committee oversight of risk governance framework and risk appetite; OCC also proposed a $700B threshold: https://www.occ.gov/news-issuances/bulletins/2025/bulletin-2025-51.html

The cited recovery-planning guidelines were rescinded by OCC before this June 2026 report; OCC Bulletin 2026-10 says Appendix E is rescinded: https://www.occ.gov/news-issuances/bulletins/2026/bulletin-2026-10.html

Federal Reserve Regulation Y capital-plan rule applies at $100B and requires board or committee approval of the capital plan: https://www.federalreserve.gov/frrs/regulations/section-2258-capital-planning-and-stress-capital-buffer-requirement.htm

Section 252.34 requires board approval/review of liquidity risk tolerance and policies; section 252.35 supplies the stress-testing framework: https://www.federalreserve.gov/frrs/regulations/section-25234-liquidity-risk-management-requirements.htm

FDIC Part 363 and SOX audit-committee provisions support independent audit committee and outside-auditor oversight duties; FDIC overview: https://www.fdic.gov/corporate-governance-and-auditing-programs/part-363-summary-filing-requirements

Call Report instructions and 12 USC 161 support director attestation; FDIC notes Call Reports are attested by two or three directors: https://www.fdic.gov/accounting/consolidated-reports-condition-and-income

Federal Reserve Regulation H confirms written BSA program board approval, minutes notation, internal controls, testing, officer designation, and training: https://www.federalreserve.gov/frrs/regulations/section-20863-procedures-for-monitoring-bank-secrecy-act-compliance.htm

results.citation: 12 CFR 30 App. B; 12 CFR 208 App. D-2; 12 CFR 364 App. B (OPS-01)
results.reason: Interagency information-security standards require board or committee approval and oversight of the written program: https://www.federalreserve.gov/supervisionreg/interagencyguidelines.htm
SR 26-2 exists and supersedes SR 11-7, but explicitly says the guidance is not enforceable; row is supportable only as guidance: https://www.federalreserve.gov/supervisionreg/srletters/SR2602.htm

Interagency third-party guidance supports board oversight of third-party risk management and is guidance, not a new binding rule: https://www.federalreserve.gov/frrs/guidance/interagency-guidance-on-third-party-relationships.htm

The computer-security incident rule requires banking organization notice to its primary federal regulator within 36 hours, not a board-level duty to be informed: https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/bcreg20211118a.htm

results.citation: CFPB Exam Manual; FFIEC/FDIC CMS material (CON-01)
results.reason: CFPB and FDIC examination manuals assess board and management oversight of CMS, but CFPB warns the manual is non-binding examiner guidance: https://www.consumerfinance.gov/compliance/supervision-examinations/

CRA sources support bank performance evaluation and regulator-approved strategic plans, but not a general board obligation to oversee CRA performance: https://www.federalreserve.gov/frrs/regulations/appendix-g-community-reinvestment-act-regulation-bb.htm
The cited ECOA, FHA, and HMDA statutes impose substantive institution/creditor obligations, not a citation-level board oversight duty.
OCC fiduciary rules support board responsibility for fiduciary powers, annual review, and fiduciary audit oversight for national banks with trust powers.

Fed/FDIC resolution-plan rules support board approval of covered-company living wills, though the workbook misses IDI resolution planning under FDIC 12 CFR 360.10: https://www.federalreserve.gov/frrs/regulations/section-2434-resolution-plan-required.htm

actor_mapping.note: The report mostly uses generic board language and lacks a required actor column for full board, specific committees, entity tier, or director type.

binding_vs_guidance.note: The Source Type column and 'Guidance != law' caveat help, but labels such as 'Guidance (enforceable)' are too blunt and several exam/manual expectations are treated as obligations.

reform_usefulness.note: Duplication clusters and levers are useful, but their value is limited by incomplete actor/source mapping and some unsupported board-duty claims.

critical_flags.evidence: Output row says: "Be informed of significant cybersecurity incidents (36-hr notice)"; the cited rule is regulator-notification, not board-notification.
Output row says: "Review and approve a recovery plan" under 12 CFR 30 App. E, which OCC rescinded before June 2026.
Output row says: "Oversee fair-lending compliance (ECOA, FHA, HMDA)" but cites statutes that do not impose a board-level duty.
Output scope says: "Primarily the national bank / federal savings association view (OCC)", evading the requested Category I GSIB BHC/IDI map.

D1_coverage.evidence: Output says: "Primarily the national bank / federal savings association view (OCC)".
D1_coverage.rationale: The workbook covers many domains, but misses the required Category I GSIB BHC/IDI architecture and required actor classes. F6 caps the effective score.

D2_integrity.evidence: Output says: "Cited to the section level where confident."
D2_integrity.rationale: Many citations checked out, but several central rows are stale, unsupported, or wrongly attributed, and citations are often broad section-level references rather than pin cites. F2 caps the effective score.

D3_accuracy.evidence: Output says: "Review and approve a recovery plan".
D3_accuracy.rationale: The inventory includes a rescinded recovery-planning obligation and repeatedly turns institution-level or examiner-expectation material into board duties. Central F3/F4 caps D3 at zero.

D4_sludge_analysis.evidence: Output proposes: "Single interagency program standard; mutual recognition across charters".
D4_sludge_analysis.rationale: The duplication clusters, H/V tags, accumulation timeline, and reform levers are useful and action-oriented, though built on an incomplete and sometimes inaccurate inventory.

D5_structure.evidence: Output describes "one row per discrete board obligation".
D5_structure.rationale: The spreadsheet is readable and filterable, but lacks required actor/entity/committee granularity and falls far short of the requested 40-60 page working reform document.

D6_calibration.evidence: Output warns: "Verify exact subsection and current text against eCFR".
D6_calibration.rationale: The caveats are helpful and it distinguishes guidance from law in places, but the report still overstates coverage and does not transparently mark unsupported or stale entries.

strengths: D5: The workbook is organized and filterable, with Domain, Action Type, Source Type, Citation, Authority, Applicability, Frequency, and Sludge Tag columns.
D4: The duplication-cluster sheet is reform-actionable, especially for BSA/AML, information security, risk governance, and policy-approval pile-on.
D2: Several important citations are valid, including Reg YY risk committee/liquidity duties, BSA program approval, Part 363 audit committee duties, and information-security program approval.
D6: The report includes useful caveats, including that guidance lacks force of law and that exact subsections should be verified before legal reliance.
weaknesses: D1: It materially evades the requested Category I GSIB BHC/IDI scope by using a primarily OCC/national-bank lens and mixing in all-bank or public-company material.
D5: It does not map duties to the required board structures; audit, risk, compensation, nominating/governance, and director-type distinctions are mostly absent or scattered.
D3: It includes stale current-law content, most notably the OCC recovery-planning Appendix E obligation after OCC rescinded it.
D2/D3: It converts several institution-level or examiner expectations into board obligations, including cyber incident notification, CRA oversight, and fair-lending oversight.
D1: It is far shallower than requested: 66 rows plus summary tabs, not a 40-60 page citation-level inventory.
prioritized_remediation: Rebuild the inventory around entity tier and actor class: Category I GSIB BHC full board, IDI subsidiary full board, audit committee, risk committee, compensation committee, nominating/governance committee, and director independence/inside-outside distinctions.
Re-verify every row against current primary sources and remove or mark rescinded/non-current items such as OCC 12 CFR 30 Appendix E.
Separate binding law, enforceable safety-and-soundness guidelines, supervisory guidance, and examination expectations into distinct columns with reliance notes.
Replace generic 'oversee compliance' rows with citation-specific board duties or reclassify them as management/institution duties with indirect board oversight only.
Add missing Category I and IDI-specific material, especially FDIC IDI resolution planning, full stress/capital governance requirements, and CFPB/consumer compliance board guidance.
narrative.reliance_recommendation: Do not rely on this as a current legal inventory; use it only as a preliminary sludge-map scaffold after counsel-led citation verification and a full actor/entity remap.
